h: int):\\n    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:\\n        return None\\n    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100\\n    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))\\n    cosine_steps = max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)\\n\\n    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.2, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_steps)\\n    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_steps)\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(optimizer, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_steps])\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Use the fixed step budget more effectively by adding a short warmup and cosine decay over the actual training horizon, which typically improves early optimization quality under tight step limits.\",\"secondary_modes\":[]}"}}
